hypothesis: At a fixed 400-case AI release-canary budget, oversampling a one-percent critical stratum will materially increase power to detect its regression, while inverse-probability weighting will remain necessary to estimate the population-average regression without selection bias.
claimBoundary: The study identifies estimator and sampling behavior under declared synthetic matched-outcome distributions. It does not validate any organization's risk labels, prescribe universal allocation weights, estimate a named model's failure rate, or replace a production shadow test.

baselines: balanced one-third-per-stratum allocation at the same budget
controls: misranked risk in which the largest regression occurs in routine traffic
null scenario with equal candidate-only and baseline-only discordance
